items.q: How do you say 'I study English at school' in Spanish?
items.misconception: Estudio el inglés a la escuela.
items.why: Students add unnecessary articles or wrong prepositions. Languages as school subjects DROP the article: 'estudio inglés', 'me gusta el inglés' (general likes need article). Location: EN (not a) for places: en el colegio, en casa, en clase.

items.q: How do you say 'My sister is embarrassed' in Spanish?
items.correct: Mi hermana tiene vergüenza / está avergonzada.
items.why: Notorious mistake when discussing family/relationships. 'Embarazada' = PREGNANT, not embarrassed. To say embarrassed: tener vergüenza or estar avergonzado/a. Saying your sister is pregnant when you mean embarrassed causes laughter.

items.q: Why does 'Me gusta el chocolate' literally translate as 'Chocolate is pleasing to me'?
items.correct: Gustar works backwards - the thing liked is the subject, the person is the indirect object.
items.misconception: Gustar means 'to like' and works exactly like English.
items.random_wrong: It is a typo for 'Yo gusto'.
items.why: Students translate gustar as 'to like' with English structure. Actually gustar = 'to be pleasing to'. So 'me gustan los libros' (books are pleasing to me) - the verb agrees with the thing, not the liker.

items.q: How many diputados sit in the Congreso?
items.why: Correct answer: 350. “230” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is the future of 'votar' for 'él'?
items.why: It's votará. “votó” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was the current Spanish Constitution adopted?
items.why: The real figure is 1978. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
